name: The Genesis Chronicle
description: Every article, manifesto, and deep dive into the Genesis Semantic Design System — from color science to AI-driven design evolution.

hasPart.headline: The Death of Utility Classes: Why Ontological CSS Changes Everything
hasPart.description: A manifesto against the utility-class revolution and a case for ontological CSS — where you describe what content IS, not how it should look.

hasPart.headline: Property Ownership: The Rule That Makes CSS Conflicts Impossible
hasPart.description: Why each CSS concern belongs to exactly one ontological category — and how that makes specificity conflicts structurally impossible.

hasPart.headline: Web Components Without the Framework Tax
hasPart.description: 17 Lit-based custom elements that enhance semantic HTML without a build step, a virtual DOM, or a runtime framework.

hasPart.headline: Design With Memory: How AI Agents Evolve a Living Design Genome
hasPart.description: Inside the system where every design decision has an origin story and AI reviewers enforce semantic purity.

hasPart.headline: Accessibility Is Not a Feature — It Is the Architecture
hasPart.description: How the Genesis ontology makes WCAG compliance a structural guarantee instead of a checklist item.

hasPart.headline: The Void and the Ethereal: Crafting Atmosphere in Code
hasPart.description: How a single word — void, ethereal, sacred, vibrant — sets the emotional tone of an entire page region.

hasPart.headline: OKLCH: The Color Space Your Eyes Were Waiting For
hasPart.description: Why hex codes lie to your brain and how perceptual uniformity changes everything about design systems.

hasPart.headline: Getting Started with the Genesis Design System
hasPart.description: From clone to first page in under ten minutes — installation, layout selection, and ontological customization.
